row_annotations.issue_summary: T. Spaccio coded Exempt on M&E account (6102). Same vendor on the same date uses the Meals and Entertainment tax code at row 51. Memo notes '(Tax Exempt)' but Exempt treatment on a meals expense suppresses ITC recovery. If this is a receipt split, the Exempt portion needs support.
row_annotations.suggested_next_step: Confirm whether this T. Spaccio transaction is genuinely exempt or should use the Meals and Entertainment tax code consistent with row 51.

row_annotations.issue_summary: Emily G (person-name vendor) billed $6,351.18 on Billable Expense coded Exempt. If Emily G is a freelancer without GST/HST registration, Exempt may be correct, but vendor registration status is needed.
row_annotations.suggested_next_step: Confirm whether Emily G is GST/HST-registered.

pattern_annotations.issue_summary: Multiple Exempt-coded transactions on the same M&E account (6102) where the same vendor appears under Meals and Entertainment tax code on the same date. Suggests bill splits where the Exempt portion may not be justified.

unmapped_observations.issue_summary: Three vendors appear only once in the workbook with unusually round dollar amounts. Not a checklist concern but may warrant a second look.
unmapped_observations.reason_not_checklist_flag: No checklist item covers vendor frequency or round-number anomalies.
unmapped_observations.suggested_follow_up: Verify invoices exist for these one-off vendors.
